title: Linear Regression (Training)
description: Implement linear regression training with gradient descent. Given X, y, learning rate, and epochs, update weights w and bias b to minimize MSE loss.
key_concept: MSE loss = (1/n) * sum((y_hat - y)^2). Gradients: dL/dw = (2/n) * X.T @ (y_hat - y), dL/db = (2/n) * sum(y_hat - y). Update: w -= lr * dw, b -= lr * db.

hints.L1: Forward: y_hat = X @ w + b. Compute MSE loss. Backward: gradient of loss w.r.t. w and b. Update w and b.
hints.L2: error = y_hat - y. dw = (2/n) * X.T @ error. db = (2/n) * sum(error). w -= lr * dw; b -= lr * db. Repeat for all epochs.
hints.L3: def train(X,y,w,b,lr,epochs): n=len(y); for _ in range(epochs): yh=X@w+b; err=yh-y; w-=lr*(2/n)*(X.T@err); b-=lr*(2/n)*err.sum(); return w,b

optimal_solution:
import numpy as np

def train_linear_regression(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, w: np.ndarray, b: float, lr: float, epochs: int):
    n = len(y)
    for _ in range(epochs):
        y_hat = np.dot(X, w) + b
        error = y_hat - y
        dw = (2/n) * np.dot(X.T, error)
        db = (2/n) * np.sum(error)
        w = w - lr * dw
        b = b - lr * db
    return w, b
